__warnings.note: DECISION: kept Shona 'Mhoro' verbatim. Rationale: 'Mhoro' is Rwen's signature greeting and part of the brand voice — Rwen IS culturally Shona, the user just reads about Rwen in Tagalog. Alternatives considered: flip to 'Mabuhay' or 'Magandang araw' (would lose brand consistency); flip to mixed 'Mabuhay (Mhoro), {{name}}!' (verbose). Reviewer: confirm or override.
Kept Shona 'Ndine urombo' verbatim ('I am sorry') — same brand-voice rationale as messages.welcome. The English chrome 'something went wrong' is translated to Tagalog.
Kept 'Mhoro!' verbatim — Rwen's signature opener for hands-free conversation.
'{{lang}} companion · AI' — '{{lang}}' is filled at runtime with the learned-language name. Translated 'companion' to 'kasama'.
Chat input placeholders — kept short and readable.

input.placeholder_convo_active: Aktibo ang usapan — magsalita o i-tap ang ⏹ para itigil

messages.welcome: Mhoro, {{name}}! Ano ang nasa isip mo ngayon?
messages.error_generic: Ndine urombo — may nangyaring mali. Subukan muli.
messages.auto_listen_greeting: Mhoro! Nakikinig ako. Magsalita kapag handa ka na.
